title: Password Hashing, Verification, and Upgrade · Failure Repair Bay
summary: An incident packet contains a plausible versioned password credential verifier with one primitive, parameter, nonce, context, key, parser, trust, timing, migration, or recovery defect; preserve the symptom and isolate first cause before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in versioned password credential verifier and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, an…
objectives: Store password verifiers with a slow password-hashing construction rather than plaintext, reversible encryption, or fast general hashes.
Select and encode Argon2id memory, time, parallelism, salt, and output parameters from current guidance and measured service budgets.
Choose scrypt or PBKDF2 only from explicit availability and compliance constraints with current calibrated parameters.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Crypto Go Password Hash Not Encrypt
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug cryptographic engineering team handles case 7726 during a credential migration window. Build deterministic Go evidence to admit a versioned password verifier, bound KDF work, and select verify-then-rehash migration; preserve accessible status, non-color meaning, keyboard operation, error recovery, and secret-safe diagnostics; then change one authenticated byte and prove clean rejection without partial effect. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Store password verifiers with a slow password-hashing construction rather than plaintext, reversible encryption, or fast general hashes.
